# Force causes acceleration

## Short Teaching Summary
The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it and inversely proportional to its mass.

## Difficulty
0.16

## Prerequisites
- Push or pull action
- Unbalanced forces cause acceleration

## Related Concepts
- Push or pull action (requires)
- Unbalanced forces cause acceleration (requires)

## Misconceptions
- None recorded.

## Best Examples
- Resultant force (F) and acceleration (a) are directly proportional; if you double the resultant force acting on an object, you double its acceleration.
- If mass is constant, the force applied is equal to the mass multiplied by the change in velocity divided by the change in time (F = m * (V1 – V0) / (t1 – t0)).

## Best Analogy
- None recorded.
